result:
**Summary of changes since last review:**

| | Count |
|---|---|
| Fixed | 2 (`bot.py` temp file leak, `azure-cosmos` removed) |
| Still present | 13 |
| New issues | 4 |

**New issues found:**
1. `main.py:68` — `Anthropic()` client recreated on every call (connection pool churn)
2. `bot.py:44,60` — `asyncio.get_event_loop()` deprecated in Python 3.12+
3. `bot.py:50` — Raw error details forwarded to Telegram users
4. `requirements.txt` — Duplicate unpinned + pinned entries (conflated hand-written and `pip freeze` output)

The most urgent unfixed item remains rotating the three live API keys in `.env`, especially since they were also captured in the `code_re_review.json` file committed to the repo root.

findings.message: Plaintext API keys (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN, POLAR_API_KEY) still visible. Rotate all three immediately — they have also been captured in the repo-root code_re_review.json artifact.
No error handling on Anthropic API calls. anthropic.APIError, RateLimitError, AuthenticationError, and network errors propagate unhandled.
No error handling on Anthropic API calls in executor. Unhandled exceptions crash the Telegram handler silently.
No URL encoding on URL construction. urllib.parse.quote() is needed for make/model slugs; special characters or non-ASCII can produce malformed or manipulable URLs.
requests.get(url) has no timeout, blocking the executor thread indefinitely if kolesa.kz is slow or unresponsive.
next() still raises StopIteration if API response contains no tool_use block. Use next(..., None) with a guard.
Webhook configured without secret_token. Anyone who discovers the URL can send forged Telegram updates.
No USER directive — container still runs as root.
pytest==9.0.3 still in production requirements. Move to requirements-dev.txt.
No User-Agent header on requests.get; python-requests fingerprint may be blocked by kolesa.kz.
POLAR_API_KEY is still present and unused in any code. Revoke and remove.
azure-core is still listed (azure-cosmos was removed). azure-core appears unused; remove it along with its transitive deps (cffi, cryptography, pycparser, isodate).
NEW: anthropic.Anthropic() client instantiated on every identify_car() call, creating a new connection pool per photo. Instantiate once at module level.

NEW: asyncio.get_event_loop().run_in_executor() is deprecated in Python 3.12+. Use asyncio.to_thread(identify_car, tmp_path) or asyncio.get_running_loop().run_in_executor() instead. Same issue at line 60.
NEW: Error message reply_text(f"Could not identify car: {result['error']}") may expose internal details (file paths, API error messages) directly to Telegram users.

NEW: File mixes unpinned top-level entries (lines 1-5) with a full pip-freeze block (lines 6-39). Redundant unpinned entries should be removed; keep only the pinned freeze output or a clean hand-curated list.
